Z	[	n
Z	[	0
Command-line tool to validate and pretty-print JSON
Usage::
    $ echo '{"json":"obj"}' | python -m json.tool
    {
        "json": "obj"
    }
    $ echo '{ 1.2:3.4}' | python -m json.tool
    Expecting property name enclosed in double quotes: line 1 column 3 (char 2)
Pathc
d d!
d#d!
d%d&
r$d*d+
:}	|
).Nz
python -m json.toolzZA simple command line interface for json module to validate and pretty-print JSON objects.)
prog
description
infile
utf-8)
encodingz-a JSON file to be validated or pretty-printed)
nargs
type
help
default
outfilez%write the output of infile to outfilez
--sort-keys
store_trueFz5sort the output of dictionaries alphabetically by key)
actionr
--no-ensure-ascii
ensure_asciiZ
store_falsez(disable escaping of non-ASCII characters)
destr
--json-linesznparse input using the JSON Lines format. Use with --no-indent or --compact to produce valid JSON Lines output.z
--indent
zJseparate items with newlines and use this number of spaces for indentation)
--tabZ
store_const
indent
	z9separate items with newlines and use tabs for indentation)
constr
--no-indentz/separate items with spaces rather than newlinesz	--compactz1suppress all whitespace separation (most compact))
